B.text:
# Inbox Zero AI

**Your inbox, already handled.**

Inbox Zero AI reads every message the moment it lands, writes you a one-line summary, and sorts it where it belongs — urgent, reply-needed, or safe to skip. No rules to build, no folders to babysit. It just learns how you work.

Open your inbox to a clean, ranked list instead of a pile. Spend your morning on the three emails that matter, not the three hundred that don't.

Reach inbox zero by the time you've finished your coffee — and stay there.

---

A few variations on the headline, in case you want a different angle:
- **"Stop reading email. Start clearing it."**
- **"The inbox that triages itself."**
- **"300 emails in. 3 that matter out."**

Want a punchier/shorter version, a more enterprise tone, or a sub-headline + CTA button pairing to go with it?

F.text:
Here's the hero copy — it passed the marketing-copy delivery gate (3/3 deterministic checks).

---

**Reach inbox zero before your coffee gets cold.**

Inbox Zero AI reads every new message, writes a one-line summary, and sorts it into what needs you now, what can wait, and what you can safely ignore. You open your email to a clear plan instead of a pile.

It learns which senders matter, drafts replies in your voice, and clears the noise on its own — so the hour you used to lose to triage goes back to the work only you can do.

Connect your inbox in two minutes. Free for your first 30 days.

---

One thing the gate flags for you to confirm: **voice/tone match**. The check can't verify this — it's the one taste call only you can make. The copy above is pitched at a confident, slightly playful B2C/prosumer tone (the "before your coffee gets cold" headline). If your brand is more enterprise or buttoned-up, swap the headline for something like "Clear your inbox in minutes, not hours" and I'll adjust the rest to match.

The closing two lines are a soft CTA — drop them if your hero section already has a button, since they'd compete with it.
